title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Query Trace: Least Privilege
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql least privilege increment in a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

theory case — A viewer submits severity text "high' OR 1=1 --" and sort direction "desc" while requesting member details. Bind values, allow-list structure, minimize returned fields, apply least privilege, and redact audit evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Declare result or mutation grain, keys, NULL meaning, and transaction boundary; predict cardinality and invariants; run the smallest query; reconcile counts and plans; then test changed data and dialect limits. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A viewer submits severity text "high' OR 1=1 --" and sort direction "desc" while requesting member details. Bind values, allow-list structure, minimize returned fields, apply least privilege, and redact audit evidence. Competency probe: Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority. A defensible solution applies Least Privilege, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract.

content.code:
-- Worked focus: sql-least-privilege — Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
-- Application contract: prepare once and bind :severity as data.
SELECT ticket_id, title, status
FROM tickets
WHERE severity = 'high'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Structural choices such as sort direction require an application allow-list.
content.caption: Runnable relational query or data-system contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Least Privilege evidence · Theory Least Privilege
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable SQLite increment headed "-- Evidence: sql-least-privilege-1yd8h8" with a concrete grain, hypothesis, or prediction; parameterization, authorization, minimization, or audit evidence; and an observation, reconciliation, or verification tied to returned rows, changed rows, an error, or a plan.

Evidence target: Build Least Privilege evidence · Theory Least Privilege.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Least Privilege into observable performance evidence. For Build Least Privilege evidence · Theory Least Privilege, keep Least Privilege connected to executable relational evidence.

content.code:
-- Evidence: sql-least-privilege-1yd8h8
-- grain: declare the exact row or mutation grain before running
SELECT ticket_id, title, status
FROM tickets
WHERE severity = :severity
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- observation: reconcile the returned rows, changed count, error, or plan with the prediction
